Universe

This text is injected into the system prompt for any story whose lead character belongs to this universe.
Quests
Design story arcs for this world: connected quests with their own locations and characters. New characters stay hidden until the player discovers them in play.
🌌
Generated with the ModelsLab model + LoRA set in Settings → Image → Special-purpose (default: juggernaut-xl-9 + background-outdoor-world-city-v2-0). Write your own prompt.
Every character below is part of this world. Open the story to roleplay — nobody is nearby until you call them in (OOC: /bring Name) or the Gamemaster brings them. Tap a card to edit a character.

v25.5
1 · API Keys
OpenRouter, ModelsLab/Atlas, fal.ai, CivitAI, Deepgram & Inworld
▶
CivitAI key lets the app attach your token to a CivitAI LoRA download link automatically (so login-gated LoRAs resolve). Paste a CivitAI download URL in a rule's LoRA path — the token is appended for you.
ModelsLab's API may block direct browser calls (CORS). If images/videos fail here, they'll work from the packaged mobile app or through a small proxy.

Voice keys (speech & TTS)

2 · LLM Selection
Every language model the app calls — one picker per function, none hidden
▶
Every LLM used anywhere in the app is selectable here. Blank fields fall back along the chain named next to them, so nothing silently uses a model you can't see. Sampling controls (Top-P / Top-K / penalties) and reasoning effort apply to roleplay replies only — engine calls (JSON parsers, judges, routers) always run on provider defaults so their output stays parseable.

Roleplay (main character replies)

Leave blank to always use the single Model above. With a list, each roleplay reply rotates to the next model and cycles back to the start — handy for mixing an expensive model with cheaper ones. The refusal fallback still applies to whichever model rotation picked.
This is the base creativity & length. Other functions fall back to these when set to Auto. Using a reasoning model (e.g. deepseek-v4-pro)? Keep this at 1200+ — reasoning models spend hidden tokens thinking before they answer, and a low limit can make them return nothing.
How hard reasoning models think, when reasoning is on above. Applies to roleplay replies only; Auto sends nothing. Models without effort levels ignore it.
The samplers below apply to roleplay replies only (solo, multi-character and gamemaster-reaction turns). Background engines are never affected — their JSON output must stay stable. Leave on Auto to use the provider's own default (nothing sent).
Comma-separated provider names to skip for every call. The same model is hosted by many providers; some wrap it in their own content filter and return a Chinese "无法…" refusal. Denying those routes you to a cleaner host (fallbacks stay on, so this never breaks a request). Leave blank for no restriction. If it seems ignored, the exact provider slug may differ from the display name — check a debug response's provider field and match it here.

Refusal fallback

When a roleplay reply comes back as a refusal, this model retries the same turn once. Previously hidden — it silently used the Director model.

Auto-RP Player Narrator

Narrates your short inputs into full player turns when Auto-RP is on.

Prompt Rewriter

Turns each reply into image & video generation prompts.

Multi-Character Director

Turn routing (who replies, who chains in), presence tracking, voice-consistency checks and the memory arc tracker.

Memory & Daily Engines

Memory builder/query/ranker/condenser, diaries, daily relationships, calendar reconcile, quests and scene recaps.

Gamemaster, Scene Writer & Judges

The hidden director: staleness judge, event author, scene writer, confrontation & overture judges.

Character Generator & Background Tasks

"Generate with AI" character cards, auto-created characters, latent NPCs, schedules, locations and tracker generation. On/off switches live in Game Preferences.

Authoring model (world & prompt building)

The strong model used for the rare, high-leverage generation jobs that shape everything downstream: building universes, writing character bios, authoring each world's director notes, and tuning the Living-Universe prompts to fit a world. It runs only a handful of times per universe — not per message — so a top-tier model here is cheap and pays off across the whole playthrough. A capable model is strongly recommended.
Universe generator length & creativity. Building a full world with many characters is a large JSON response. If a generated universe fails to appear, raise Max tokens here — a 9-character world needs plenty of room. (Sampling penalties never apply to this job, so the JSON structure can't be derailed.)

Image/Video Router

Reads each scene and picks which image/video model rule to use (rules live in Image / Video Settings).

Voice Calls & Speech

The live-call reply model. A fast model matters — first-word latency is what you feel. Provider routing is set to lowest-latency automatically.
Corrects mis-heard words in your speech before the character replies (toggle lives in TTS Settings → call tuning).
3 · Payloads
Every instruction package the AI receives — with a placeholder guide, pickers, and movable placeholder blocks
▶

How payloads & placeholders work

A payload is an ordered stack of parts. Text parts (solid border) are prompts you edit in place — each one is its own box below. Placeholder parts (dashed purple border) are filled with live story data at send time; their fixed sentences are editable, the live data itself is the story. Placeholder parts can be moved with ▲▼, deleted with ✕, and re-added with the ＋ Placeholder button on each payload. Inside any text you can also use {{token}} placeholders — the {{ }} button on every text box lists the tokens that work there and inserts them at the cursor.
Placeholders are NOT universal. Each token is filled only by the specific engine that sends that prompt — a token pasted into a prompt whose engine doesn't fill it is sent to the model as literal {{text}}. That's why each box's picker only offers its own supported tokens — an empty picker means that prompt gets no substitutions at all. {{user}} (your name) is the most widely supported token, but even it only works where a picker lists it — plus in story text you author yourself (universe setting, character cards, plan titles), which is always name-substituted.
Engine payloads: every text part is editable, but their part order is fixed in code — engine outputs are parsed as JSON and the assembly is code-defined. Only the three reply payloads support reordering, deleting and re-adding parts.
Placeholder dictionary — every token, what it means, where it works

Reply payloads

The messages a roleplay reply model receives, as an ordered part list — use ▲▼ to reorder, ✕ to remove a placeholder part, ＋ Placeholder to bring one back. Parts below the DIALOGUE marker are delivered after the chat history (closest to generation — strongest influence; moving live facts above the dialogue increases drift). Order is global for all universes.

Engine payloads

The background jobs — routers, memory, relationships, director engines, generators, image/video writers. Every prompt is edited here in place (saved instantly, with reset, Write with AI and a placeholder picker). Part order is fixed for engines — see the guide above.
4 · Image Settings
Provider, model rules, auto-illustration & every special-purpose image model
▶
Used by any rule set to “Default”. Each rule below can override this and use its own provider — so e.g. standard scenes can run on AtlasCloud while intimate scenes run on ModelsLab.
Used when a rule has no model id of its own.
Smart routing — let an LLM pick the rule per scene
When on, the router reads each scene and chooses a rule below. When off, the first enabled rule is always used. (The router's LLM is picked in LLM Selection.)

fal.ai (z-image LoRA)

A second image provider with z-image + LoRA. Set a rule's provider to "fal.ai" to use it; the rule's LoRA path/scale apply here too. Key is in the API Keys section.

Auto-Illustration

Auto-generate scene image each reply
On = every reply is illustrated automatically. Off = each reply shows a Generate image button.

Special-purpose image models

These jobs used to run on hidden, hardcoded models — all of them are selectable now. Blank = the default image provider/model above.
5 · Video Settings
Animate & Extend models, motion rules, length, resolution & sound
▶

Base video models

Animate runs on wan-2.2 turbo-spicy image-to-video-lora and makes the initial clip from a scene image — the routed video rule's LoRAs and trigger keyword apply. Extend (the reel button on later scenes) runs on wan-2.5 video-extend: it takes that image's prompt, writes a "scene continues…" prompt sized to the length below, and appends new footage onto the chat's latest clip — press it again and again to keep growing the video.

Animate — wan-2.2 turbo-spicy options (initial clip)

These control the Animate button (wan-2.2-turbo-spicy image-to-video-lora) that seeds the first video. The routed video rule's LoRAs load into the model's high/low-noise channels; the output follows the scene image's own aspect ratio. This model renders 5s or 8s clips — the duration slider snaps to the nearest. Set a wan-2.5 model id below to get 5–20s durations and voice-guided audio instead.

Extend — wan-2.5 video-extend options (Movie button)

The reel/Movie button on a later scene extends the chat's latest video. It writes a "scene continues…" prompt from that image (paced to the duration below) and appends this many seconds of new footage onto the end of the current clip. Each press lengthens the same growing video — the newest result always becomes the base for the next extend. Needs an initial Animate clip first. The extend prompt writer is editable in Settings → Payloads → Video / Movie Prompt Writers.
Used by any rule set to “Default”. Each rule below can override this with its own provider.
Used when a rule has no model id of its own. Image-to-video: the scene image is the first frame.
Smart routing applies to video too (toggle is shared with images).
From 480p up to 1024p — 720p is the standard middle option. Higher resolution costs more and takes longer.
Generate sound (WAN audio)
When supported by the video model (e.g. WAN 2.x i2v), requests audio along with the clip.
6 · TTS Settings
Dubbing, narration, voices & live-call speech tuning
▶

Dubbing — spoken replies & talking videos (xAI TTS)

Powers the speak button on chat replies, diary Read-aloud, and talking videos. Runs on your AtlasCloud key. Each character can override the voice in their editor.

Live-call voice & turn-taking

Real-time spoken conversations with a character. Speech-to-text by Deepgram, the reply by your call model (LLM Selection → Voice Calls), and the voice by Inworld through your TTS relay. See DEPLOY-WORKER.md for the relay.
Voice & turn-taking
Raise Utterance end if the character jumps in before you finish a long sentence. Raise Speaking rate / use STABLE delivery if speech feels too slow or pausey.
Exact whole-word match (Turkish-aware): abi=âbi fixes “abi” but never touches “tabi”. Applied to the text just before it’s spoken.
Auto-fix speech-to-text errors
When on, a quick model corrects mis-heard words in YOUR speech using the conversation context before the character replies. More accurate, but adds a small delay before each reply. Leave off for fastest responses. The fixer model is picked in LLM Selection.
7 · Game Preferences
Every gameplay toggle, threshold & slider — plus identity and appearance
▶

You (global defaults)

Used when a universe hasn't set its own player identity. Each universe can override these in its editor.
When a scene image includes you, this description is used so you look consistent.

Roleplay flow

Dynamic presence — story moves characters in/out
When a character clearly leaves or arrives, they exit/enter the scene automatically.
Relationship tracking
Characters build feelings (-100…+100) toward each other and you, fed back into the story.
Freeform trackers
Per-universe values (love, pregnancy, trust…) with staged consequences. Define them in the Universe editor.
Voice consistency check
Flags clearly out-of-character replies with a hint. Costs one extra small call per reply.
Scene recap on resume
Shows a “Previously…” line when you reopen a chat after a break.

Memory

Enable memory system
Builds memories every 10 messages, then retrieves the most relevant ones each turn. Models live in LLM Selection; prompts live in Payloads.
A finished memory arc is only stored if its importance reaches this. Higher = fewer, more significant memories (raise it and trivial chatter is dropped). Keep it well below 0.55 — the offstage intent engine reads memories at that importance to decide who forms grudges/loyalties, so a high floor can starve it. Charged bystander glimpses (for gossip) are exempt.
Enable condenser
When on, a character's oldest memories are clustered by similar context and merged into durable long-term memories once they pass the ceiling below.
Long-term memory
When a character's raw memories reach the ceiling, the ones OLDER than the newest N are clustered by similar context and the similar ones are merged into durable long-term memories. Diaries are short standalone entries and are never condensed.
Recall. Each turn the app injects a few of the character's most relevant memories (semantically searched across their whole memory bank) plus a few diary / long-term entries, under separate headings.
Semantic memory (neural embeddings)
Recall memories by MEANING, not just keywords — each memory is embedded and ranked by vector similarity, using your OpenRouter key (no separate key needed). Off, or if a call fails, retrieval falls back to lexical matching automatically.
The embeddings model is picked in 2 · LLM Selection → Memory & Daily Engines (blank = openai/text-embedding-3-small). Every embeddings request — and each turn's weighted memory-retrieval ranking — is logged on the Debug page.
Retrieval weights — how much each facet counts when ranking which memories to recall (0 = ignore that facet):
How many of the most recent messages go into each roleplay request. Lower = cheaper & faster, but the AI remembers less of the immediate scene (older facts are still covered by Memory). Default 20.
Strip narration from old messages
Trims *narration* from OLDER messages within the window to save tokens. Recent messages are always sent in full. Applies only to roleplay replies.
Private narration (subjective scene)
Each present character only witnesses their OWN *narration* and {{user}}'s — never another character's narrated actions/thoughts (they still hear everyone's spoken dialogue). Stops characters reacting to things they couldn't have seen. Off = everyone sees all narration (old behaviour).

Living Universe

When characters are present but not in your conversation, they catch a vague impression of who you were with and how it looked. At End Day, charged glimpses (flirtation, arguments, secrecy) can travel through the relationship graph to people who'd care — planted as a vague rumor and an open suspicion in their head, which colors your next scene with them. Most glimpses spread to no one.
Enable gossip
Confrontations (gossip → someone comes to settle it)
When a character holds a strong, unresolved suspicion about you, they may arrive to confront you. The Scene Writer runs it as a winnable social check: a hidden conviction the evidence sets a floor on, that your excuses, consistency, and honesty move — while their personality decides how movable they are.
Offstage agency (characters form grudges/ambitions & act on them)
Characters who are wronged, tempted, or see an opening form private motives — a grievance, ambition, scheme — and pursue them offstage between days: festering, recruiting allies who share the sentiment. When one is ready, it comes back as a confrontation. You insult someone; days later they return, and not always alone.

Gamemaster & scenes

An invisible director. Every few messages it checks whether the scene has gone stale and, if so, slips the character a hidden cue to act on.
Enable Gamemaster
Encounters now happen DURING the day: every N of your turns, two co-located offstage characters may interact — charged (a confrontation, a scheme) or casual (tea, a film, bumping into each other at the bar). The event physically places them at its venue, so you can go there and walk in on the aftermath; they remember it. Higher N = a quieter world.
Enable Scene Writer
After the Gamemaster triggers an event, the Scene Writer drives it to a real resolution — escalating if ignored and bringing approaching characters in at the right moment.

Characters

Auto-create introduced characters
When the Gamemaster brings in a new character, add a card to the current universe immediately, then fill its bio after a few messages.

World & time

How much in-game time a trip costs by default. Each location can override this.
Calendar / plan detection
Tracks future meetings, promises, and conditional commitments characters make so they aren't forgotten. You can edit or delete any entry.

Appearance

Roll responses in as they appear
Animates each reply into the chat word-by-word instead of pasting it all at once. Higher = faster roll.
Scales the whole app — make everything smaller or larger.
8 · Data & Backup
Storage, export/import, snapshots & clear
▶

Storage & Cleanup

Storage used—
Full backup
Automatic snapshots
The app quietly keeps the last 3 snapshots of everything on this device. If something goes wrong, restore one here.
Roleplay (this universe's chats + memories)
Universes (worlds + characters, no chats)
Prompts & settings (prompt pack)
Danger zone
Debug — AI payloads
Every request sent to an AI service is logged here (newest first) — including embeddings calls and a local Memory retrieval trace per turn showing each candidate memory's per-facet scores under your weights. Tap a row to see the full payload. API keys are redacted. Export saves the latest payloads as a JSON file you can send for diagnosis.

Generate Universe

Describe the world you want. The AI builds the setting, your character's background, and a ready-to-play cast. Pictures aren't generated — add them per character afterward.

New Universe

A universe groups characters who share the same world. Its setting is added to every roleplay played inside it.
Rewrites the living-engine prompts with this world's flavor: gossip, intent form/tick, confrontation judge, offstage interactions, plan executor, goal pursuit, and the character-quest designer / step / text-ask. Each gets a prompt-specific updater brief; every result is validity-checked (placeholders, JSON contract, code-evaluated enums like the quest gate menu) and skipped if it would break anything. Saved as per-universe overrides — your global prompts stay untouched. Manual only — never runs automatically.
Chronicle — 0 entries
Edit the six sections + universe guide
Your character in this universe
Leave blank to use your global defaults from Settings.
At the start of each day (End Day) you return here. Leave as "Stay where I am" to wake wherever you ended the night.
Trackers
Named numeric values the story tracks (suspicion, intoxication, a power budget, pregnancy…). Each can belong to the whole story, a single character (private to them), or you the player (public or private). Each tracker has its own behaviour, its own AI prompt & model, and breaking points. Values start fresh in each chat.
Locations
Places in this world — homes where characters live and points of interest to travel to. You can generate a picture for each; the chat background changes when you're there.
Builds each character's daily rhythm across these locations. Save the universe first if you just added locations.
Prompts (this universe)
Override any system prompt just for this universe. Anything left on Default uses your global prompt from Settings. Tap Edit to customize, or Write with AI to generate one from a short request.

Edit prompt

Write this prompt with AI

The assistant writes a complete system prompt from your request. If this prompt must return structured JSON, that requirement is added automatically — you don't need to mention it.

Edit message

Add memory

Call a character

Pick who to talk to. You'll have a real-time spoken conversation — speak, and they answer aloud.

Last call debug

🎭

…

Connecting…
0:00
In-person conversation · speak naturally · metered services (Deepgram · OpenRouter · Inworld)

Edit location

Public places (a café, a club, the marina) talk — leave one and a rumor about your visit may travel. Homes should be 0: what happens at home stays private. Higher = leakier.
Characters ticked here are present when you arrive at this place — and are never placed anywhere else automatically. They're also this place's regulars: when it leaks gossip (above), the rumor reaches every one of them — including the person it's about.
Each area is its own earshot — someone who steps to the toilet won't hear talk in the lounge. The Entrance is where everyone arrives and can't be removed.

Tracker

Story = one shared value everyone sees. You = the player. Characters = a private copy for each one you pick.
Plain English. Each turn the AI reads the recent scene and nudges the value based on this.
hit on
Leave the dice blank for a guaranteed hit. 1d3 hit-on-3 ≈ 33%. 1d2 hit-on-2 ≈ 50%. 1d4 hit-on-4 ≈ 25%.
The value jumps here, then the daily count below takes over.
Added (or subtracted) automatically when you press End Day. "Only after value reaches" delays counting until the value is at least that — e.g. pregnancy starts counting only once conceived.
Its own model
A dedicated AI model for this tracker (optional)
▶
Becomes public knowledge
Past a threshold, others come to know it — vaguely
▶
Once the value crosses the threshold, this vague line is shown to everyone who can perceive it and planted as durable knowledge — each character reads it through their own relationship to the person (a stranger assumes the obvious; someone who knows better reacts differently). The owner still privately sees the exact stage above.

Set time & place

Who's nearby?

Every character in this universe is part of the cast. Only those marked nearby hear you and can respond. Bring someone nearby or send them away here, or use OOC commands in chat: /bring Name, /leave Name, /who. With nobody nearby, you're narrating alone until the Gamemaster or you bring someone in.

New Character

Global base instruction — applies to every character
These principles (emotional authenticity, push-and-pull, conflict, restraint…) are automatically applied to all characters before their individual details below. You don't need to add them here. Edit them in Settings → Global base instruction.

    

    

      

        
 Create with AI
Describe the character; the LLM fills the card.

        
      

      

        
        

          

            
            
          

          

            
            
          

        

        
        
Fills the fields below — you can edit anything before saving.

      

    

    

    

      
Which world this character belongs to. The universe's setting is added to their stories.

    

      

      

        
        
      

      
Uses the Appearance field below + your ModelsLab image settings. Fill in Appearance first for best results.

    

    

    

    

    

    

      
      
A systematic personality skeleton so the character behaves consistently across all five axes — not just their one or two loud traits. Generated by " Create with AI" or written by hand.

    

      
      
Baseline motivations the character pursues on their own — read by the Gamemaster, the offstage-intent engine, and the character's own turns, so they drive the story instead of only reacting.

    

    

    

      

        
        
      

      
Pick a Turkish or multilingual voice, or paste a custom/cloned id. Blank = global default voice.

    

    

      
Structured so back/profile shots can drop the face cleanly. Leave blank to inherit nothing — fill what you know. WRITE IN ENGLISH.

      

        

        

      

      

        

        

      

      
      

      

      
    

    

    

      
Hooks for the director engines — what destabilizes this character or raises the stakes for them. Shared with the Gamemaster and Scene Writer (never shown to other characters as dialogue).

    

      
This character's map of their world — fed into their payload so they act on real ties (kinship, hierarchy, rivalry, jealousy). Your text is never overwritten; the engines may append things discovered in play below.

      

    

      
      

        
      

      
      
Distinct from the numeric feeling tracker — this is durable social fact. First run uses only the character cards; later runs fold in how the long-term opinions have changed.

    

    
 Where they live: assign this character as a resident of a place in the Universe → Locations editor. Homes they live in are always part of their day.

    

      

    

    

      

      

        
      

    

      
 Relationships & standing
